steps.title: Convert the growth phrase
steps.content:
"$150\%$ more" means the new amount is the original amount plus $150\%$ of the original:
$$
1+1.50=2.50
$$
So the yearly growth factor is $2.5$.

steps.title: Find the starting value
steps.content:
At the end of $2019$, $t=1$ and the population is $175$. Divide by one growth factor to get the value at $t=0$:
$$
\frac{175}{2.5}=70
$$

steps.title: Write the model
steps.content: The model has starting value $70$ and growth factor $2.5$: $$ n=70(2.5)^t $$ The answer is B.
